This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|View of the West Kofun (left rear) from the East Kofun}}]] {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|label={{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|Location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as other_designations by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a other_designation by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a other_designation by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | ISO = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|View of the West Kofun (left rear) from the East Kofun}}]] {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|label={{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}. Three of the mounds have been designated as other_designations by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was designated a other_designation by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was designated as a other_designation by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | 規模 = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | 埋葬施設 = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | 出土品 = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Early 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial Burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the artifacts recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, artifacts included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary Burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ}}). ** Artifacts found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | 別名 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳}} | 所属 = | 所在地 = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | 形状 = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} | 規模 = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | 埋葬施設 = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | 出土品 = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | 築造時期 = Late 5th century | 被葬者 = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | 特記事項 = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its dimensions are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == other_designation == === Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation === * other_designation ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated other_designation List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == {{small|(Sources used for writing this article)}} * other_designation information board (set up by the former Tokinami Village Board of Education) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 30: Place names in Nara Prefecture|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=「Sanryobo Nishi Kofun」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}. **** {{Wikicite|reference=Later edition (collected in Japan Knowledge), 2006|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}. *** {{Wikicite|reference=「Sanryobo Higashi Kofun」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}. **** {{Wikicite|reference=Later edition (collected in Japan Knowledge), 2006|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}. * Hiroyuki, "Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun" |ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}, {{Wikicite|reference=Hashimoto Hiroyuki, "Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun" |ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}. * {{Cite Fumihide, "Sanryobo Nishi Kofun" |ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}}. == Related Literature == {{small|(Related literature not used in writing this article)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun – Preliminary Survey Report|publisher=Tokinami Village Board of Education|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor={{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所}}|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|publisher=Tokinami
