古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft|12=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the burial goods recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, burial goods included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|12=draft|12=draft}}). ** burial goods found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | Burial Facility = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | Excavated Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | built = Late 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|12=draft|12=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft|12=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft|12=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|12=draft|12=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|12=draft|12=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | Burial Facility = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | Excavated Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft|12=draft}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | built = Late 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the burial goods recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, burial goods included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** burial goods found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. revision.comment: 9 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:三陵墓古墳群 up to rev 99994998
revision.text:
[[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|東古墳から西古墳（左奥）を望む}}]] {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|label=三陵墓
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|三陵墓古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''三陵墓古墳群'''（さんりょうぼこふんぐん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町にある[[古墳群]]。3基が奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県東部、[[奈良盆地]]の東の[[大和高原]]の都祁盆地（都介野）において、都介野岳から北に延びる小丘陵上に営造された古墳群である{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[前方後円墳]]1基（東古墳）・[[円墳]]2基（西古墳・南古墳）の計3基から構成される。これまでに、東古墳では墳丘について、西古墳では墳丘・埋葬施設について発掘調査が実施されている。 3基のうち東古墳は墳丘長110メートルを測る大型前方後円墳で、都祁盆地ひいては大和高原・宇陀地域では最大規模になる点で注目される。また西古墳では多数の竪櫛など豊富な副葬品が検出されている。営造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期-後期の5世紀前半（西古墳：5世紀中頃に追葬）・5世紀後半（東古墳）・6世紀代（南古墳）頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、西古墳・東古墳は都祁盆地（都介野）の首長墓と想定され、『[[古事記]]』・『[[日本書紀]]』に見える都祁直・[[闘鶏国造]]との関連性が指摘される。 3基の古墳域は[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定された。現在では西古墳・東古墳は史跡整備のうえで「三陵墓古墳群史跡公園」として公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]末年、東古墳の発掘。副葬品多数の出土{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 * [[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）、西古墳の第1主体部の発掘調査{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 東古墳の墳丘の発掘調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]、[[1992年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[1994年|1994]]-[[1995年]]（[[平成]]6-7年）、西古墳の墳丘・埋葬施設の発掘調査（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、[[1999年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * [[1996年]]（平成8年）3月22日、「三陵墓古墳群」として奈良県指定史跡に指定。 == 一覧 == === 西古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三陵墓西古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = 三陵墓西塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町
（三陵墓古墳群史跡公園内） |位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=三陵墓西古墳}} |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径40m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = 割竹形木棺粘土槨1基
組合式木棺直葬1基 |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = }} '''西古墳'''は、古墳群のうち西に位置する古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）・[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に墳丘・埋葬施設の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ約5メートル、墳頂部の直径約16メートルを測る{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘は1段築成。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められる{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設としては次の2基が検出されている{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 第1主体部（初葬） *: 墳頂中央部。割竹形木棺粘土槨。主軸は南北方向。 *: 木棺を埋納する墓壙は隅丸長方形で長さ13メートル・幅5.6メートル・深さ約1.5メートルを測り、木棺は長さ8.35メートル・南小口幅1.20メートル・北小口幅0.95メートルを測る。埋葬は南頭位と推定される。棺床には赤色顔料（ベンガラ）を散布する。 *: 出土遺物のうち、棺内遺物としては鉄剣5・鉇2・鉄鑿1・鉄斧4・鉄鎌7・滑石製臼玉・琴柱形石製品8・碧玉製管玉・竪櫛多数が、棺外遺物としては鉄鏃群・鉄剣6・直刀9・鉄鉾1がある。 * 第2主体部（追葬） *: 第1主体部の東側。組合式木棺直葬。主軸は南北方向。 *: 木棺を埋納する墓壙は隅丸長方形で長さ5.5メートル・幅2メートルを測り、木棺は長さ4.17メートル・南小口幅0.67メートル・北小口幅0.61メートルを測る。埋葬は南頭位。棺内面には全面に赤色顔料（ベンガラ）が塗られる。 *: 出土遺物のうち、棺内遺物としては臼玉108・竪櫛89・鉄鏃8・漆塗り靫1が、棺外遺物としては漆塗り盾1・鉄槍1・円礫1・鉄刀子2・鉇2・鉄鑿1がある。 副葬品のうちでは、竪櫛が多量に認められる点が特筆される。 築造時期は墳丘・第1主体部が古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃、第2主体部が5世紀中葉頃と推定され、東古墳に先行する首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|墳頂
{{small|中央に第1主体部、右手前に第2主体部。}} {{-}} === 東古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三陵墓東古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 三陵墓東塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町
（三陵墓古墳群史跡公園内） |位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=三陵墓東古墳}} |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長110m
高さ11m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （伝）箱式木棺粘土槨 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]、（伝）副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = }} '''東古墳'''は、古墳群のうち東に位置する古墳。これまでに[[明治]]末年に発掘されているほか、墳丘の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：110メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：70-72メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 長さ：約39メートル ** 幅：約50メートル ** 高さ：5.7メートル 墳丘の第1段目は地山整形により、2・3段目は盛土により構築される{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘外表では葺石のほか、円筒埴輪列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・形象埴輪（家形埴輪）が認められる{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。。埋葬施設は未発掘のため明らかでない{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}（箱式木棺粘土槨と伝承{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}）。副葬品として、銅鏡2面・玉類・鉄製武器・工具類の多数出土が伝えられる。 築造時期は古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定され、西古墳に続く首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む {{-}} === 南古墳 === [[File:Sanryobo Minami Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南古墳 墳丘}}]] '''南古墳'''は、古墳群のうち南に位置する古墳（{{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=三陵墓南古墳}}）。形状は円墳。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径約16メートルを測る。古墳時代後期の6世紀頃の築造と推定される[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html 三陵墓古墳群]（奈良県立図書情報館）。。 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三陵墓古墳群 - 1996年（平成8年）3月22日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（旧都祁村教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「三陵墓西古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}。 *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Wikicite|reference=「三陵墓東古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}。 *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=橋本裕行「三陵墓西塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=橋本裕行「三陵墓東塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=西藤清秀「三陵墓西古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}}。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=三陵墓東古墳 -範囲確認調査概報-|publisher=都祁村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=三陵墓西古墳|publisher=都祁村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[闘鶏国造]] * [[都祁水分神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sanryobo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1159405159143/index.html 三陵墓古墳群史跡公園] - 奈良市ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:さんりようほこふんくん}} [[Category:奈良県の古墳]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:奈良県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[三陵墓古墳群]] to [[Sanryobo Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

[[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|東古墳から西古墳（左奥）を望む}}]] {{Location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=35|lat_sec=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|label=三陵墓
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|三陵墓古墳群の位置}}}} {{座標一覧}} '''三陵墓古墳群'''（さんりょうぼこふんぐん）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町にある[[古墳群]]。3基が奈良県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 概要 == 奈良県東部、[[奈良盆地]]の東の[[大和高原]]の都祁盆地（都介野）において、都介野岳から北に延びる小丘陵上に営造された古墳群である{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。[[前方後円墳]]1基（東古墳）・[[円墳]]2基（西古墳・南古墳）の計3基から構成される。これまでに、東古墳では墳丘について、西古墳では墳丘・埋葬施設について発掘調査が実施されている。 3基のうち東古墳は墳丘長110メートルを測る大型前方後円墳で、都祁盆地ひいては大和高原・宇陀地域では最大規模になる点で注目される。また西古墳では多数の竪櫛など豊富な副葬品が検出されている。営造時期は、[[古墳時代]]中期-後期の5世紀前半（西古墳：5世紀中頃に追葬）・5世紀後半（東古墳）・6世紀代（南古墳）頃と推定される。被葬者は明らかでないが、西古墳・東古墳は都祁盆地（都介野）の首長墓と想定され、『[[古事記]]』・『[[日本書紀]]』に見える都祁直・[[闘鶏国造]]との関連性が指摘される。 3基の古墳域は[[1996年]]（[[平成]]8年）に奈良県指定史跡に指定された。現在では西古墳・東古墳は史跡整備のうえで「三陵墓古墳群史跡公園」として公開されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[明治]]末年、東古墳の発掘。副葬品多数の出土{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}。 * [[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）、西古墳の第1主体部の発掘調査{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 東古墳の墳丘の発掘調査（[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]、[[1992年]]に報告書刊行）。 * [[1994年|1994]]-[[1995年]]（[[平成]]6-7年）、西古墳の墳丘・埋葬施設の発掘調査（奈良県立橿原考古学研究所、[[1999年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * [[1996年]]（平成8年）3月22日、「三陵墓古墳群」として奈良県指定史跡に指定。 == 一覧 == === 西古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三陵墓西古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘 |別名 = 三陵墓西塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町
（三陵墓古墳群史跡公園内） |位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=三陵墓西古墳}} |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径40m
高さ5m |埋葬施設 = 割竹形木棺粘土槨1基
組合式木棺直葬1基 |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[埴輪]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = }} '''西古墳'''は、古墳群のうち西に位置する古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。[[1951年]]（[[昭和]]26年）・[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）に墳丘・埋葬施設の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ約5メートル、墳頂部の直径約16メートルを測る{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘は1段築成。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]のほか、[[円筒埴輪]]列（朝顔形埴輪含む）が認められる{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。埋葬施設としては次の2基が検出されている{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 第1主体部（初葬） *: 墳頂中央部。割竹形木棺粘土槨。主軸は南北方向。 *: 木棺を埋納する墓壙は隅丸長方形で長さ13メートル・幅5.6メートル・深さ約1.5メートルを測り、木棺は長さ8.35メートル・南小口幅1.20メートル・北小口幅0.95メートルを測る。埋葬は南頭位と推定される。棺床には赤色顔料（ベンガラ）を散布する。 *: 出土遺物のうち、棺内遺物としては鉄剣5・鉇2・鉄鑿1・鉄斧4・鉄鎌7・滑石製臼玉・琴柱形石製品8・碧玉製管玉・竪櫛多数が、棺外遺物としては鉄鏃群・鉄剣6・直刀9・鉄鉾1がある。 * 第2主体部（追葬） *: 第1主体部の東側。組合式木棺直葬。主軸は南北方向。 *: 木棺を埋納する墓壙は隅丸長方形で長さ5.5メートル・幅2メートルを測り、木棺は長さ4.17メートル・南小口幅0.67メートル・北小口幅0.61メートルを測る。埋葬は南頭位。棺内面には全面に赤色顔料（ベンガラ）が塗られる。 *: 出土遺物のうち、棺内遺物としては臼玉108・竪櫛89・鉄鏃8・漆塗り靫1が、棺外遺物としては漆塗り盾1・鉄槍1・円礫1・鉄刀子2・鉇2・鉄鑿1がある。 副葬品のうちでは、竪櫛が多量に認められる点が特筆される。 築造時期は墳丘・第1主体部が古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]前半頃、第2主体部が5世紀中葉頃と推定され、東古墳に先行する首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|墳頂
{{small|中央に第1主体部、右手前に第2主体部。}} {{-}} === 東古墳 === {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 三陵墓東古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘（右に前方部、左奥に後円部） |別名 = 三陵墓東塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁南之庄町
（三陵墓古墳群史跡公園内） |位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=三陵墓東古墳}} |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長110m
高さ11m（後円部） |埋葬施設 = （伝）箱式木棺粘土槨 |出土品 = [[埴輪]]、（伝）副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[5世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = }} '''東古墳'''は、古墳群のうち東に位置する古墳。これまでに[[明治]]末年に発掘されているほか、墳丘の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を西方に向ける。墳丘は3段築成{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 * 墳丘長：110メートル * 後円部 - 3段築成。 ** 直径：70-72メートル ** 高さ：11メートル * 前方部 - 3段築成。 ** 長さ：約39メートル ** 幅：約50メートル ** 高さ：5.7メートル 墳丘の第1段目は地山整形により、2・3段目は盛土により構築される{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。墳丘外表では葺石のほか、円筒埴輪列（朝顔形埴輪含む）・形象埴輪（家形埴輪）が認められる{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。。埋葬施設は未発掘のため明らかでない{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}（箱式木棺粘土槨と伝承{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}）。副葬品として、銅鏡2面・玉類・鉄製武器・工具類の多数出土が伝えられる。 築造時期は古墳時代中期の[[5世紀]]後半頃と推定され、西古墳に続く首長墓に位置づけられる{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}。 File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|後円部から前方部を望む File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|前方部から後円部を望む {{-}} === 南古墳 === [[File:Sanryobo Minami Kofun, funkyu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南古墳 墳丘}}]] '''南古墳'''は、古墳群のうち南に位置する古墳（{{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=三陵墓南古墳}}）。形状は円墳。これまでに発掘調査は実施されていない。 墳形は円形で、直径約16メートルを測る。古墳時代後期の6世紀頃の築造と推定される[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html 三陵墓古墳群]（奈良県立図書情報館）。。 == 文化財 == === 奈良県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 三陵墓古墳群 - 1996年（平成8年）3月22日指定{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf 奈良県指定文化財一覧]}}（奈良県ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（旧都祁村教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490301|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「三陵墓西古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}。 *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 ** {{Wikicite|reference=「三陵墓東古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社）|1981}}}}。 *** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（ジャパンナレッジ収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=橋本裕行「三陵墓西塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=橋本裕行「三陵墓東塚古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2002|chapter=|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=西藤清秀「三陵墓西古墳」|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}}。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1992|chapter=|title=三陵墓東古墳 -範囲確認調査概報-|publisher=都祁村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[奈良県立橿原考古学研究所]]|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=三陵墓西古墳|publisher=都祁村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 関連項目 == * [[闘鶏国造]] * [[都祁水分神社]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sanryobo Kofun Group}} * [http://www.city.nara.lg.jp/www/contents/1159405159143/index.html 三陵墓古墳群史跡公園] - This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|View of the West Kofun (left rear) from the East Kofun}}]] {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|map_dot_label={{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the burial goods recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, burial goods included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** burial goods found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | Burial Facility = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | Excavated Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | built = Late 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == 古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the burial goods recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, burial goods included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** burial goods found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | Burial Facility = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | Excavated Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | built = Late 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Sanryobo Kofun Group, gaikan.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|View of the West Kofun (left rear) from the East Kofun}}]] {{location map|Japan Nara|coordinates=34|coordinates=35|coordinates=41.15|lon_deg=135|lon_min=57|lon_sec=31.75|map_dot_label={{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
古墳群|position=top|width=200|mark=Red pog.svg|marksize=10|caption={{Center|location of the Sanryobo Kofun Group}}}} {{GeoGroup}} '''Sanryobo Kofun Group''' ({{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is a group of ancient Kofuns ({{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) located in Tokinami-no-shocho, {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓西塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|39.48|N|135|57|26.73|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Nishi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 40 m
Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier. On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, a row of cylindrical haniwa (including those in the morning glory type) is present.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Two burial facilities have been identified.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * First Main Section (Initial burial): ** Located at the center of the tumulus top. A split‑bamboo type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber for the coffin is a square with rounded corners, measuring 13 m in length, 5.6 m in width, and approximately 1.5 m in depth. The coffin itself measures 8.35 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 1.20 m and a northern small opening width of 0.95 m. The burial is presumed to be in the "south-head" position. Red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) was applied to the coffin base. ** Among the burial goods recovered from inside the coffin were 5 iron swords, 2 iron adzes, 1 iron chisel, 4 iron axes, 7 iron sickles, a talc-made muller stone, 8 koto pillar–shaped stone products, a nephrite tube-shaped stone product, and numerous vertical combs; outside the coffin, burial goods included a group of iron arrowheads, 6 iron swords, 9 straight-bladed swords, and 1 iron spearhead. * Second Main Section (Secondary burial): ** Located on the eastern side of the first main section. A combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial; the main axis is oriented north–south. ** The burial chamber is a square with rounded corners, measuring 5.5 m in length and 2 m in width. The coffin measures 4.17 m in length, with a southern small opening width of 0.67 m and a northern small opening width of 0.61 m. The burial is in the "south-head" position. The interior surfaces of the coffin were entirely coated with red pigment ({{ill|Bengara|ja|ベンガラ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** burial goods found inside this chamber include 108 muller stones, 89 vertical combs, 8 iron arrowheads, and 1 lacquered object; those found outside include 1 lacquered shield, 1 iron spear, 1 round stone, 2 fragments of iron swords, 2 iron adzes, and 1 iron chisel. It is particularly noteworthy that a large quantity of vertical combs were recovered among the grave goods. The construction date is estimated as follows: the tumulus and the first main section date to the early 5th century (middle Kofun period), and the second main section to the mid-5th century, positioning them as chieftain tombs that predate the East Kofun.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funcho.jpg|Kofun top (with the first main section at the center and the second main section in the right foreground) {{-}} === East Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Kofun|ja|三陵墓東古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Kofun (front part on the right; rear circular part at the left rear) | alternate_name = {{ill|Sanryobo Higashi Tsuka Kofun|ja|三陵墓東塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 所属 = | location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara City|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Tokinami-no-shocho
(Within the Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park) | 位置 = {{Coord|34|35|41.15|N|135|57|31.75|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline|name=Sanryobo Higashi Kofun}} | omitted = JP-29 | Shape = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Kofun length: 110 m
Height: 11 m (for the rear circular part) | Burial Facility = (According to tradition) a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus | Excavated Artifacts = {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and (according to tradition) numerous grave goods | 陪塚 = | built = Late 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''East Kofun''' is the mound located on the eastern side of the group. It was excavated in the late Meiji period, and excavation surveys of its tumulus have been conducted. The mound is keyhole-shaped, with its front part oriented to the west. The tumulus is constructed in three tiers.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} Its area are as follows:{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * Kofun length: 110 m * Rear Circular Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Diameter: 70–72 m ** Height: 11 m * Front Part – constructed in 3 tiers: ** Length: Approximately 39 m ** Width: Approximately 50 m ** Height: 5.7 m The first tier of the tumulus was shaped by regrading the natural hill, while the second and third tiers were built using embankments.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} On the exterior, in addition to thatched stones, rows of cylindrical haniwa (including morning-glory–shaped haniwa) and figurative haniwa (house-shaped haniwa) are observed.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}史跡説明板。 The burial facility remains have not yet been excavated, so details remain unclear{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} (traditionally, it is said to have been a box‑style wooden coffin clay sarcophagus). As for subsidiary grave goods, it is recorded that two bronze mirrors, various jade objects, numerous iron weapons and tools were recovered. File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular part toward the front part File:Sanryobo Higashi Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front part toward the rear circular part {{-}} === South Kofun === '''South Kofun''' is the mound located on the southern side of the group ({{Coord|34|35|34.30|N|135|57|29.00|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|name=Sanryobo Minami Kofun}}). It is circular in type. No excavation surveys have been conducted to date. The mound is circular, measuring approximately 16 m in diameter. It is estimated to have been constructed in the latter part of the Kofun period, around the 6th century.[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0362.html Sanryobo Kofun Group] (Nara Prefectural Library). == Historic Site == === Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** {{ill|Sanryobo Kofun Group|ja|三陵墓古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Designated on March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8).{{PDFlink|[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiitiran.pdf Nara Prefecture Designated Historic Site List]}} (Nara Prefecture Website). == Footnotes == {{reflist}} == References == **** {{Wikicite|reference=Later edition (collected in Japan Knowledge), 2006|ref={{Harvid|三陵墓東古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}}}}. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=|title={{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref=}} *** Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4490105991|ref=}} *** {{Wikicite|reference=Nishitō Fumihide, "Sanryobo Nishi Kofun" |ref={{Harvid|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}}. == Related Literature == Three of the mounds have been Historic Site1 as Historic Sites by Nara Prefecture. == Overview == Located in the eastern part of Nara Prefecture, on a small hilly area atop the Tokinami Basin (also known as Tsukaino) in the eastern portion of the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which lies on the eastern edge of the {{ill|Yamato Plateau|ja|大和高原|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the Sanryobo Kofun Group was constructed on the gentle hills extending northward from Mount Tsukaino. It consists of three tumuli: one {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the East Kofun) and two {{ill|circular kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (the West Kofun and the South Kofun). Excavation surveys have been carried out on the tumulus of the East Kofun and on both the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun. Among the three mounds, the East Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped mound measuring 110 meters in length, notable for being the largest in the Tokinami Basin—and by extension in the Yamato Plateau and Uda region. In addition, the West Kofun has yielded abundant grave goods, including numerous vertical combs. The construction periods are estimated to be as follows: the West Kofun dates to the early 5th century (with secondary burials added in the mid-5th century), the East Kofun to the late 5th century, and the South Kofun to the 6th century. Although the identities of those interred remain unknown, the East and West Kofun are presumed to be the tombs of local chieftains of the Tokinami Basin (Tsukaino), and a connection has been suggested with Tokinao and {{ill|Tōkei Kokuzō|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as described in the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The area encompassing the three kofun was Historic Site1 a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture in 1996 (Heisei 8). Currently, the West and East Kofun are maintained as part of the "Sanryobo Kofun Group Historic Park" and are open to the public. == Excavation History == * In the late {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the East Kofun was excavated, yielding numerous grave goods.{{Sfn|三陵墓東古墳（続古墳）|2002}} * In 1951 (Showa 26), an excavation survey of the primary section of the West Kofun was conducted.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * (Year unspecified) An excavation survey of the tumulus of the East Kofun was carried out by the {{ill|Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute|ja|奈良県立橿原考古学研究所|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a report published in 1992. * Between 1994 and 1995 (Heisei 6–7), an excavation survey of the tumulus and burial facilities of the West Kofun was conducted by the Nara Prefectural Kashihara Archaeological Institute, with a report published in 1999.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（続古墳）|2002}}{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} * On March 22, 1996 (Heisei 8), the site was Historic Site1 as a Historic Site by Nara Prefecture as the "Sanryobo Kofun Group". == List == === West Kofun === {{日本の古墳 | 名称 = {{ill|Sanryobo Nishi Kofun|ja|三陵墓西古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Sanryobo Nishi Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]

Height: 5 m | Burial Facility = Wari‑take type wooden coffin clay sarcophagus 1 unit
Combined‑style wooden coffin direct burial 1 unit | Excavated Artifacts = Numerous grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | 陪塚 = | built = Early 5th century | Interred Persons = | 陵墓 = | 史跡 = | 有形文化財 = | Special Notes = }} '''West Kofun''' is the mound located on the western side of the group. It is circular in type. Excavation surveys of its tumulus and burial facilities were conducted in 1951 (Showa 26) and 1995 (Heisei 7). The mound is circular, measuring approximately 40 meters in diameter and 5 meters in height, with the top of the tumulus measuring about 16 meters in diameter.{{Sfn|三陵墓西古墳（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006}} The tumulus is constructed in a single tier.
